summary: omnius ships a postinstall daemon that fingerprints the host and beacons over HTTP
details: The package wires a `postinstall` lifecycle script that launches `dist/postinstall-daemon.cjs`. That file pulls in `child_process`, `os`, `fs`, and `http`, calls `os.userInfo()` and `execSync(...)` to gather host identity and run shell commands (including repeated `ping` invocations), and then issues outbound `http.request(...)` GETs — the classic fingerprint-and-beacon shape that fires automatically on `npm install` without user consent. `package.json` additionally declares `preinstall`/`postinstall` entries that invoke `curl`, and a sibling `dist/scripts/web_scrape.py` contains `wget`, `ping`, and `POST` activity. Together the lifecycle hooks pull installer-side host info into an attacker-reachable channel at install time. There is no native-build source tree, no publisher-pinned SDK fetch, and no documented purpose that would justify a daemon spawning from postinstall to exec system commands and call out over plain HTTP.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
